title: telephium maximum
intro: Telephium maximum, also known as Foxglove or Digitalis purpurea, is a plant species that belongs to the foxglove family (Scrophulariaceae). The Telephium maximum plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its cardiac glycosides which have been shown to have a positive effect on heart conditions such as atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including lowering blood pressure, improving cardiac function, and reducing symptoms of heart failure. The herbal preparations of Telephium maximum include tinctures, capsules, and topical creams, which are often used to treat heart conditions under medical supervision.
